stories.story_title: Competitive Landscape Analysis: [CLIENT_A]'s Roadmap Alignment with Market Needs
stories.story_type: Competitive insights and Existing solutions from Customer calls
stories.story_full:
## Competitive Landscape Analysis: [CLIENT_A]'s Roadmap Alignment with Market Needs

The retrieved information reveals several competitor products and features mentioned by industry guests across various events and discussions. [CLIENT_A]'s roadmap appears to address many of these competitive offerings through its evaluation intelligence platform, focusing on AI reliability, observability, and protection capabilities. This analysis examines how [CLIENT_A]'s current and planned features align with competitive market needs.

### Competitive Products & Features Mentioned

#### AI Evaluation & Observability Tools

Several competitors and alternative approaches to AI evaluation were mentioned across discussions:

1. **Traditional Evaluation Methods**
   - Manual eyeballing of outputs: "*eyeballing is not evaluation*" was highlighted as a quote from an event, indicating the industry's move beyond manual review (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Closing Remarks: The Path Ahead)
   - Traditional metrics like BLEU score and ROUGE that "*fall flat*" for modern LLM evaluation (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Executive Roundtable: The Enterprise AI Adoption Journey)

2. **Vector Databases & RAG Solutions**
   - WV8 was mentioned as a vector database partner that [CLIENT_A] integrates with (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Boost LLM Accuracy with Synthetic Data and Evaluation Intelligence)
   - Vector databases were discussed as becoming more affordable alternatives to large context windows (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Applied AI: Lessons from Deploying AI at Enterprise Scale)

3. **Fine-tuning Platforms**
   - Prabas was mentioned as a developer platform for fine-tuning LLMs (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Lessons from Deploying AI at Enterprise Scale)
   - Ludwig (for training) and Lorax (for efficient serving) were referenced as open-source projects (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Lessons from Deploying AI at Enterprise Scale)

4. **Agent Development Platforms**
   - Dust (dust.tt) was mentioned as an AI agent client that Mirakl is using (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Customer Tickets: 2025Q2 - 27274)
   - AGNTCY integration was referenced in relation to Space Agent app development (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: DevRel: Technical Partnerships - 27093)

5. **Multimodal Capabilities**
   - Competitors are developing speech-to-speech models and streaming voice capabilities (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Lessons from Deploying AI at Enterprise Scale)
   - Video and multimodal capabilities were mentioned as upcoming competitive vectors (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Lessons from Deploying AI at Enterprise Scale)

### [CLIENT_A]'s Roadmap Alignment

[CLIENT_A]'s roadmap appears to address these competitive offerings through several key initiatives:

#### 1. Comprehensive Evaluation Intelligence

[CLIENT_A]'s Luna evaluation suite provides metrics backed by AI research that are tailored to specific business use cases:

> "Under the wood for all of this is our Luna evaluation Suite which is which are metrics backed by AI research and but are tailor fit to your business" (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Productionize 2.0 Keynote & Kickoff)

The platform offers:
- **Auto-adaptive Luna evals** with continuous learning through human feedback (CLHF) workflow (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Productionize 2.0 Keynote & Kickoff)
- **Context adherence metrics** to detect hallucinations (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Productionize 2.0 Keynote & Kickoff)

#### 2. Multimodal Support Development

[CLIENT_A] is actively developing multimodal capabilities:

- A multimodal tracing design is in progress (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Multimodal - 30786)
- Engineering scoping for Multimodal MVP is underway (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Multimodal - 30786)
- Plans for audio and image metrics are in the Applied Data Science backlog (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Applied Data Science Backlog - 32957)

#### 3. Integration with Agent Frameworks

[CLIENT_A] is working on agent evaluation capabilities:

- The roadmap includes "Automated Finetuning of Llama models" (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Applied Data Science Backlog - 32957)
- Integration with AGNTCY for Space Agent app is planned (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: DevRel: Technical Partnerships - 27093)
- Weather Vibes App with AGNTCY integration has been completed (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: DevRel: Technical Partnerships - 27093)

#### 4. Human Feedback & Ratings

[CLIENT_A] has developed human ratings features to complement automated evaluations:

- The platform allows configuration of different rating types including thumbs up/down, star ratings, scores, and category ratings (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: How to Add Human Ratings to Real-Time LLM Outputs 👍)
- These ratings can be applied to LLM outputs in real-time (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: How to Add Human Ratings to Real-Time LLM Outputs 👍)

#### 5. End-to-End AI Lifecycle Support

[CLIENT_A]'s roadmap includes comprehensive support across the AI development lifecycle:

- **Evaluate**: Pre-production experimentation and evaluation (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Boost LLM Accuracy with Synthetic Data and Evaluation Intelligence)
- **Observe**: Production monitoring and debugging (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Boost LLM Accuracy with Synthetic Data and Evaluation Intelligence)
- **Protect**: Real-time guardrails for production systems (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Boost LLM Accuracy with Synthetic Data and Evaluation Intelligence)

### Customer Adoption & Integration

[CLIENT_A]'s platform is being adopted by major enterprises:

- Twilio is using [CLIENT_A] Observe as their "main thing" for engineering and product teams to find bugs in RAG and parallel tool calling (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Productionize 2.0 Keynote & Kickoff)
- Chegg reported "a growing adoption by 35 Plus team members across five teams" using [CLIENT_A] for agent tracing and evaluation (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Empowering Chegg: Building Agentic Workflows with [CLIENT_A])
- Verizon is processing approximately "5 million traces a day" with [CLIENT_A] (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Evaluation Agents: Exploring the Next Frontier of GenAI Evals)

### Gaps & Future Opportunities

Some areas where [CLIENT_A]'s roadmap could further address competitive offerings:

1. **Cost Management & FinOps**
   - Verizon has requested features for budgeting, forecasting, and cost reporting across projects (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Customer Tickets: 2025Q2 - 27274_6)
   - Competitors are focusing on cost optimization for LLM inference (Source Type: Transcripts, File Title: Applied AI: Lessons from Deploying AI at Enterprise Scale)

2. **Integration with Additional Agent Frameworks**
   - Mirakl has requested integration with Dust agent client (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Customer Tickets: 2025Q2 - 27274)

3. **Migration & Cross-Platform Support**
   - Work is underway on migration capabilities from G1.0 to G2.0 (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Migration - 30391)
   - Cross-linking between Fine-tuning/NLP studio and [CLIENT_A] 2.0 is planned (Source Type: Shortcut, File Title: Migration - 30391)

[CLIENT_A]'s roadmap demonstrates a comprehensive approach to addressing competitive offerings in the AI evaluation and observability space, with particular strength in evaluation intelligence, human feedback integration, and end-to-end AI lifecycle support. The company continues to develop capabilities in multimodal support and agent framework integration to stay competitive in the rapidly evolving market.

Story 1:
  Story ID: 32413
  Title: Automated Finetuning of Llama models
  Description: This is a new feature which will allow users to directly finetune llama models for their custom ask based off 2 inputs - 
1. Clear definition of the metric
2. Well balanced, high quality test dataset
3. [Optional] training data

The main challenge here is the creation of training data from the above pieces. The rest of the stuff can easily be automated. Hence we want to break this down into 2 parts - 
1. Where the pipeline also takes training data as required input and rest of the steps are automated
2. Use an LLM / Agent to create training data (atleast for simpler safety / security metrics), and fully automate

  Story ID: 32754
  Title: [Evaluate][promptquality] Enable dataset-level metadata for Datasets
  Description: Coveo is looking for a metadata at the Dataset level so that users can upload datasets and designate if that Dataset should be pulled into their automated testing framework. 

The automated testing framework would be a separate app that queries new datasets, and determines if the new dataset should be pulled through their RAG + chat completion flow.
  Type: feature
  Status: 500009713

  Followers: Minh Le, Minh Le, Setu Shah

  Comments: Setu to follow up with Minh.; I thought this already exists?

[@jonah](shortcutapp://members/65f87ed7-1660-4d65-b1bf-b2f348958546) : Do you know?; CI/CD and user-led evals are the most important aspects of Coveo's POV with us. Some of that is custom metrics and some of it is Datasets.

Since our Playground experience doesn't have access to their stack (tbf, none of our competitors do either) they are building pipelines to enable users to get evaluation fulfillment through dataset upload, and to weigh in on the CI/CD pipeline that tests for regressions.

They are looking for a platform that would enable their automated testing framework to automatically differentiate between which datasets should be part of these processes on a one-time (prompt comparisons, model comparisons, etc) and ongoing basis (CI/CD, this would be dynamic as well as datasets are pulled in/out).; [@minhle0718](shortcutapp://members/67eacbe8-6915-46f6-aa54-1ecd0d847efc) : Can you help elaborate why this is urgent and important? The ticket / Slack doesn't explain why this is absolutely required and not just a nice to have.; cc [@soumyamohan](shortcutapp://members/66e9ecdf-b23c-4160-891e-5e3f6a89a776); What's the reasoning for needing metadata? How does this unblock the CI / CD flow?

https://[CLIENT_A]teamworkspace.slack.com/archives/C08L6G42W9F/p1749489789148439; It helps to enhance their CI/CD flow by allowing users (both technical and non-technical) to upload a new dataset through the UI and an eval kicks off automatically.

The dataset can also be added to their growing list of datasets to eval against for all future changes. This would be determined by the metadata.

Due to lack of Dataset versioning and ability to extend existing Datasets through additional uploads into a dataset in the UI...

Story 1:
  Story ID: 32522
  Title: [promptquality] Custom metrics (in-app) running synchronously against the logged workflows
  Description: Coveo has defined their in-house LLMAJ metrics as custom metrics (in-app).

Since our custom metrics running synchronously against the logged workflows as part of EvaluateRun.finish(), it is taking ~18 mins to complete these custom metrics.

Could we make the row-base scorer function run async, perhaps as a separate optional parameter?
  Type: feature
  Status: 500004696
  Owners: No owners assigned
  Followers: Minh Le, Minh Le, Setu Shah
  VCS Branch: feature/sc-32522/-promptquality-custom-metrics-in-app-running
  Estimate: Not estimated
  Priority: No priority set
  Created Date: 2025-06-05T17:25:45Z
  Updated Date: 2025-06-13T18:43:51Z
  Completed Date: Not completed
  Days in Development: 9
  Comments: In Coveo's case, they have 1000 workflows that they log. That produces an EvaluateRun object with 1000 items in the EvaluateRun.workflows list.

When they run EvaluateRun.finish(), their custom metric executor (backed by API call for LLM completion) runs synchronously and one-by-one on the 1000 separate workflows. Then, once all executors are complete, the scores are passed to the aggregator. This particular custom metric process is taking 18 mins for them.

Since the 1000 workflows are all separate, could we run multiple executors (of the same custom metric calculation) at once to reduce not the total execution time, but time to completion?; It is important to them, and I think this is an improvement that is generalizable, especially for CI/CD where evaluation runs are automated and operate on a larger scale.

They have built a workaround that moves the custom metric workload to the logging portion and logs it as a metadata in the node, and then the executor simply returns the metadata. Per Tobi, it seems like a violation of how custom metrics are suppose to work.; Potentially. Is this a dealbreaker though? I get the progress bar request but this seems like something that isn't high priority?; I don't follow?

Even if they run async, that doesn't change the _execution_ time, it just moves that to a background thread.; Thanks Setu for the feedback.

After the requirements are met - and then going into the custom metrics step - could the executor run async on the workflows of EvaluateRun.workflows?; [@milankovacic8457](shortcutapp://members/67b7971b-eb34-46d8-8e94-c2d0e562657d) : Lower priority but we can take this on when we get free from other things.; Sure, but we've been asked to _not_ make feature additions to 1.0.

> it seems like a viol...

Story 2:
  Story ID: 22007
  Title: [Observe] Allow passing cost as attribute in add_llm method of StepWithChildren class (WorkflowStep)
  Description: ### Context
Which customer did this come from? 
What are they trying to use [CLIENT_A] for? 
What are they doing today instead? 
How important is this to them, and why?

This is a request from SentinelOne. They want to use [CLIENT_A] Evaluate to bolster their growing evaluation suite for their RAG/agent workflows that all use in-house models. Today they render this information in excel, pulled from calculations they perform themselves.

Capturing latency and cost on these steps within [CLIENT_A] is very important for them, as it directly supports reducing the tool sprawl they have and centralizing all info about their evaluation of their RAG/agent pipelines into a single place. 

EDITS from April on this: Reddit is requesting the same thing, but for Observe. 

---

### The Ask
What are they asking for? 

They are asking for the ability to capture a provided cost attribute to llm workflow steps they will be using in the PoV, and subsequently as customers. We already provide an attribute for latency (`duration_ns`), we do not have a similar attribute f...

stories.story_title: Technical Reliability and Provider Efficiency: Critical Paths to Platform Growth
stories.story_full:
# Executive Summary: Customer Pain Points & Solution - Unmet Needs

## Situation Overview
[CLIENT_B]'s teletherapy platform is experiencing rapid growth with 74% of sessions now conducted virtually, yet faces critical challenges in user experience and technical reliability. Internal data shows a 40% drop-off between initial assessment and first appointment booking, indicating significant friction in the onboarding process.

> "The virtual sessions work great for my busy schedule. I appreciate being able to see my therapist from home." - James T., Client

## Key Findings

- **Technical Reliability Issues**
  - Platform experiencing video quality problems, particularly during peak afternoon sessions
  - Internal documentation reveals 50% target reduction in video-related support tickets
  > "No specific error messages, but therapists report client video feeds freezing intermittently" - Tech Support Team

- **Provider Productivity Challenges**
  - Combined no-show (14%) and cancellation (19%) rates significantly impact therapist satisfaction
  - Therapists spending excessive time on post-session documentation
  > "Expected 30% reduction in post-session administrative time with planned enhancements" - Features Documentation

- **Client-Provider Matching Optimization**
  - 78% therapy session completion rate indicates room for improvement
  - Recent uptick in support tickets regarding matching algorithm effectiveness
  > "It took a while to find someone who was accepting new patients and had availability that worked with my schedule" - Sarah M., Client

## Strategic Implications
The platform must balance rapid growth (15,000 new users/month) with service quality. With 89% of patients reporting significant improvement, focusing on technical reliability and matching efficiency could unlock substantial growth potential and improve the current 4.5-month average user retention.

## Recommendations
1. Implement immediate technical infrastructure upgrades to address video stability, particularly during peak hours
2. Deploy automated note-taking assistance to reduce therapist administrative burden
3. Enhance matching algorithm transparency and incorporate availability preferences more prominently

## Risk Considerations
Platform stability during scaling presents the primary risk, as evidenced by recent outages during high-traffic periods. Implementation of load balancing and performance monitoring systems is essential to mitigate these risks.

stories.story_title: Client Segments: Challenges and Strategic Initiatives
stories.story_full:
## Client Segments: Challenges and Strategic Initiatives

[CLIENT_B] serves diverse client segments with unique needs, preferences, and challenges. Analysis of user data reveals distinct patterns in engagement, preferences, and treatment outcomes across different demographic groups.

### Key Client Segments and Their Characteristics

#### Young Adults (18-25)

This segment demonstrates specific preferences and engagement patterns:

* **73% prefer therapists specializing in anxiety and depression**
* **82% opt for evening or weekend availability** (Coda: Therapist and Clients/Client Intake & Matching Overview)

Young adults represent a significant growth opportunity, particularly as they show high engagement with digital tools. However, they face challenges with platform technical issues, as evidenced by Noah Patel's experience with profile updates: *"I'm trying to change my phone number, but every time I click save, it just reverts back to the old one. It's really frustrating because now my therapist can't reach me."* (Dialpad: Profile Update Technical Issue - April, Noah Patel)

#### LGBTQIA+ Clients

This segment shows distinct engagement patterns:

* **89% prioritize therapists with specific LGBTQIA+ competency**
* **61% engage in weekly sessions**, compared to 48% of the general population (Coda: Therapist and Clients/Client Intake & Matching Overview)

The high prioritization of specialized competency indicates the importance of matching algorithms that account for cultural competence and specialized training.

#### Veterans

Veterans demonstrate unique preferences in therapy delivery:

* **92% match with trauma-informed therapists**
* **76% prefer in-person sessions**, significantly higher than the overall average (Coda: Therapist and Clients/Client Intake & Matching Overview)

This preference for in-person sessions contrasts with the overall trend toward telehealth, which now accounts for **68% of all appointments**, up from 62% in Q3 (Coda: Therapist and Clients/Client Intake & Matching Overview).

### Common Challenges Across Segments

#### Technical Platform Issues

Multiple clients have reported technical difficulties that impact their therapy experience:

1. **Profile Update Problems**: *"I'm trying to change my phone number, but every time I click save, it just reverts back to the old one."* (Dialpad: Profile Update Technical Issue - April, Noah Patel)

2. **Video Session Connectivity**: *"I can't seem to join my video therapy session and it's supposed to start in 5 minutes!"* (Dialpad: Video Session Technical Connection Issue - Danielle, Alice Reed)

3. **Scheduling System Errors**: *"I've been trying to book an appointment with my therapist for the past couple of days, but every time I click to confirm, I get an error message."* (Dialpad: Platform Scheduling Error Prevents Booking - Joe, Emma Foster)

These technical issues have led some clients to consider competitor platforms: *"I was considering switching to Headspace Health because of these issues, but I'm glad it's sorted now."* (Dialpad: Platform Scheduling Error Prevents Booking - Joe, Emma Foster)

#### Between-Session Support Gap

Analysis reveals a critical engagement gap between therapy sessions:

* **47% decline in static content usage** over 2 years
* **312% increase in support-seeking behavior** through messaging and chat features
* **78% of clients feel particularly unsupported during critical evening hours (7-10pm)** when therapists are unavailable
* This percentage rises to **89% among younger demographics** (Coda: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach PRD/[CLIENT_B] AI Coach - Between-Session Support)

### Ongoing Strategic Initiatives

#### AI Coach for Between-Session Support

To address the support gap, [CLIENT_B] is developing an AI-powered coaching system with:

1. **Contextual Conversation Engine** leveraging natural language processing optimized for mental health terminology
2. **Dynamic Content Generation** for personalized coping exercises and interactive CBT worksheets
3. **Therapist Oversight Dashboard** for real-time monitoring of client interactions
4. **Crisis Detection & Response** with 24/7 monitoring and automated risk assessment (Coda: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach PRD/[CLIENT_B] AI Coach - Between-Session Support)

Success metrics include increasing weekly active users from **45% baseline to 80%** and reducing therapy dropout rates from **32% to 16%** (Coda: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach PRD/[CLIENT_B] AI Coach - Between-Session Support).

#### Matching Algorithm Enhancement

[CLIENT_B] is improving its proprietary matching algorithm to better connect clients with appropriate therapists:

* Incorporating machine learning techniques to analyze historical data on successful matches
* Integrating more granular data points on therapist specialties and client preferences
* Implementing A/B testing to continuously refine matching criteria (Coda: Annual Planning/[CLIENT_B] Annual Strategic Planning Workbook 2025)

Recent optimizations have yielded:
* **22% reduction in algorithm processing time**
* **18% increase in first-match acceptance rate**
* **9% decrease in requests for therapist reassignment** (Coda: Therapist and Clients/Client Intake & Matching Overview)

#### Regional Market Expansion

[CLIENT_B] is strategically expanding into new regions based on market analysis:

| Region | Market Size | Current Penetration | Growth Potential | Competitive Landscape |
|--------|------------|---------------------|------------------|------------------------|
| Southeast | $39M | 11% | Medium | Moderate |
| Midwest | $33M | 9% | High | Low |
| Pacific Northwest | $35M | 25% | Low | High |
| Southwest | $88M | 19% | Medium | Moderate |
| Northeast | $88M | 14% | Low | High |


(Coda: Annual Planning/[CLIENT_B] Annual Strategic Planning Workbook 2025)

The Midwest expansion is a priority due to high growth potential and low competition, with targeted marketing campaigns, partnerships with local healthcare providers, and increased therapist recruitment efforts.

### Client Feedback and Satisfaction

Recent client testimonials highlight both strengths and areas for improvement:

**Positive Experiences**:
* *"I've had two sessions so far with my therapist and one with my psychiatrist. My insurance has already been billed for my first two sessions. It appears the copays are typical for my insurance. The therapist matching process was quick and I'm pleased with my provider so far."* - Corrine C. (March 2025)
* *"The virtual sessions work great for my busy schedule. I appreciate being able to see my therapist from home. The platform is easy to use and I haven't had any technical issues."* - James T. (February 2025) (Coda: User Feedback Doc/[CLIENT_B] User Feedback & Insight Log)

**Areas for Improvement**:
* *"I wish there were more therapists available in my area. It took a while to find someone who was accepting new patients and had availability that worked with my schedule."* - Sarah M. (April 2025)
* *"The billing process was confusing at first. It would be helpful to have a more detailed breakdown of costs upfront."* - Michael R. (March 2025) (Coda: User Feedback Doc/[CLIENT_B] User Feedback & Insight Log)

These insights are driving action items including conducting provider surveys, enhancing onboarding materials, and developing strategies to increase therapist recruitment in underserved areas.

User Story 3: AI-Driven Suggestion and Analytics
================================================

The product managers do a lot of work in CODA like creating product requirements documents (PRD), creating and maintaing product roadmap, and product briefs. Other than that they usually manage the user research in CODA and also use it to store some user analytics reports. We want to create a product, a sort of chrome extension, that could help the product managers in doing their work easily. Some use-cases on these lines are -

The chrome extension works using AI and is based on LLM models that has access to all the documents of the company on CODA other than the general knowledge base

This PRD defines the requirements for a new Chrome extension designed specifically for product managers using CODA. The extension will streamline the process of creating and maintaining PRDs, roadmaps, and product briefs by leveraging AI and LLM models to intelligently integrate company documents, user research, and real-time analytics. Key insights indicate that PMs frequently work with scattered documents and require a tool that automatically enriches and consolidates data, thus improving speed and consistency.

Problem Statement:

Product Managers (PMs) using CODA spend significant time manually consolidating information from multiple documents, including PRDs, product roadmaps, and user research reports. This fragmented approach leads to inefficiencies, last-minute scrambles before sprint planning, and inconsistent feedback cycles.

User Needs:

PRD Draft Creation Time

Feedback Integration Time

User Adoption Rate

Document Consolidation Accuracy

This Chrome extension is poised to fundamentally enhance the productivity of product managers in CODA by automating document consolidation, facilitating real-time collaboration, and providing actionable AI-powered insights. Through targeted metrics and continuous feedback, we will iterate on the product to ensure it meets the evolving needs of our PM community, ultimately driving faster, more strategic decision-making and improving overall product quality.

"Empowering PMs with the tools to work smarter rather than harder is the cornerstone of our strategic product vision." (Source: Coda Product Context)

• PM has to finish a PRD, and keeps on making small additions whenever they get time. Eventually one day before the sprint planning, they scrappily put the pieces together and share with the engineering team
• PM has created a PRD and needs feedback from the product head.
• A new PM wants to understand the current product modules and related customer interviews & research. They are given a repository of multiple n-pager documents to access this information
• There are multiple moonshot ideas sitting in the backlog of a PM. They usually start a document or ticket to remind them that they have to work on it
• PM has received some requirements from business stakeholders like customer success and sales team. They want to triangulate the business requirements by adding voice of customers and validating if the customers/users also validate those requirements
• PM is working on this new product feature of 'live tracking' dashboard for the tele-sales team. They have written a PRD draft and now have to create wireframes to give the complete PRD to the design team so that final screens could be designed

• Streamlined Document Consolidation: Automatically pull and organize relevant data from multiple CODA documents into a single working PRD.
• Real-Time Feedback Integration: Allow real-time collection and incorporation of feedback from product heads and cross-functional teams.
• Intelligent Suggestions: Utilize AI to recommend content additions, user metrics, and trends from historical documents (e.g., user research and analytics reports).
• Ease of Navigation: Provide quick access to archived customer interviews, market research, and past PRDs so that new PMs can quickly learn about product modules.
• Idea Management: Enable easy capture and tracking of moonshot ideas and preliminary requirements from various stakeholders (e.g., customer success and sales teams).

• Automatic Aggregation: The extension will automatically scan CODA workspaces and consolidate relevant documents (PRDs, product briefs, user research) into a unified template.
• Smart Sections: Pre-defined sections based on PM workflows (e.g., problem statement, feature details, user feedback) will be auto-populated using AI.
• Keyword Triggering: Use keyword detection (e.g., âPRD draftâ, âuser researchâ) to determine which documents and metrics to include.

• Inline Comments and Suggestions: Allow users to insert comments and suggestions directly into the PRD with live visibility (e.g., "need more detailed user metrics" as noted by senior PMs) (Source: Coda Product Context).
• Notification System: Automated alerts when new feedback is received from stakeholders, ensuring timely updates.

• Dashboard View: A central dashboard showcasing recent PRDs, pending feedback, and ideas in the backlog.
• Search & Filter: Robust search functionality to locate specific documents or research material quickly.
• Wireframe Integration: Ability to attach or create wireframes directly from the extension for clarification on new features.

• Contextual Suggestions: AI will analyze previous documents for trends and provide suggestions to improve current PRD drafts.
• Data Visualization: Render key metrics and trends via markdown tables and charts. For instance:

• Voice of Customer Analysis: Integrate feedback from multiple channels to triangulate business requirements with user research.

• As a PM, I want the extension to automatically consolidate my latest documents into a draft PRD so that I can focus on refining content rather than gathering data.

• When activated, the extension scans designated CODA folders and creates a preliminary draft using pre-defined sections.The draft includes auto-populated sections for problem statements, feature descriptions, and user insights.PM can manually edit and save the draft.

Supporting User Feedback

Primary Goal: Develop an AI-powered coaching system that provides personalized, contextual support between therapy sessions, especially mental health content.

Our longitudinal analysis has revealed a critical engagement gap between therapy sessions, significantly impacting treatment outcomes. Weâve observed a 47% decline in static content usage over 2 years, while simultaneously seeing a 312% increase in support-seeking behavior through messaging and chat features. User research indicates 78% of clients feel particularly unsupported during critical evening hours (7â10pm) when therapists are unavailable, with this percentage rising to 89% among younger demographics. The objective is to bridge this support gap while maintaining therapeutic alliance and improving engagement metrics, ultimately reducing therapy dropout rates which currently stand at 32%.

Key Statistics

Active Therapy Clients (18â45)

Demographics:

1. Contextual Conversation Engine

2. Dynamic Content Generation

3. Therapist Oversight Dashboard

4. Crisis Detection & Response

Phase 3: General Availability (Q4 2024)

User Engagement

Clinical Outcomes

Client Testimonials

## Summary

This PRD enhancement evaluates the AI coach therapy approach to address the core problem that users do not feel understood by the AI. Despite its potential to deliver 24/7, personalized support and improve engagement, there are significant risks around user trust and the lack of genuine empathetic connection.

---

## 1. Clear Problem Statement and User Needs

- **Problem Statement**: Users report that the AI coach fails to truly understand their emotional nuances, leading to diminished engagement and a reduced sense of therapeutic alliance. This is particularly acute during critical evening hours when clients are most vulnerable and cannot access immediate human support.- **User Needs**: - Immediate, personalized guidance on coping strategies and mental health techniques. - A system that feels empathetic and understands the user's unique emotional state. - Reliable 24/7 support to bridge the service gaps, especially during therapist unavailability (notably, *78% of clients feel unsupported between 7â10pm* as cited in the document). - Integration with therapy session notes to reinforce continuity and personalized treatment.

(Source: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach â Between-Session Support)

---

## 2. Detailed Feature Specifications and Requirements

**Feature 1: Contextual Conversation Engine**

- *Requirements*: - Leverages natural language processing (NLP) optimized for mental health terminology. - Provides personalized responses tailored to individual therapy goals and current emotional state. - Integrates with session notes and homework assignments to enrich conversational context. - Tracks progress with built-in mood monitoring and performance tracking.

**Feature 2: Dynamic Content Generation**

- *Requirements*: - Generates personalized coping exercises, guided meditations, and interactive CBT worksheets. - Presents psychoeducational content with progress visualizations.

**Feature 3: Therapist Oversight Dashboard**

- *Requirements*: - Offers real-time monitoring of client interactions and flags concerning patterns for human intervention. - Summarizes session preparation and tracks outcome measurements.

**Feature 4: Crisis Detection & Response**

- *Requirements*: - Implements 24/7 crisis monitoring with automated risk assessment algorithms. - Escalates high-risk situations to crisis support hotlines and notifies therapists promptly.

(Source: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach â Between-Session Support)

---

## 3. User Stories and Acceptance Criteria

**User Story 1: Immediate Guidance**

- *As a user, I want immediate guidance on coping skills so that I can manage my emotional state during off-hours.* - **Acceptance Criteria**: - The system responds under 2 seconds with personalized coping advice. - Guidance is tailored to my therapy goals and integrates with session insights.

**User Story 2: Personalized Empathy**

- *As a user, I need the AI to respond in a manner that makes me feel understood and empathized with.* - **Acceptance Criteria**: - The AI displays adaptive language that resonates with my emotional state. - Users report a measurable increase in perceived empathy (target: **+25% improvement** in user sentiment surveys). - *"I often feel stuck when practicing new coping skills; having immediate guidance would be game-changing."* (Source: Client Testimonial)

**User Story 3: 24/7 Availability**

- *As a user, I want round-the-clock support that fills the gap when therapists are unavailable.* - **Acceptance Criteria**: - The system is accessible 24/7 with **99.9% uptime**. - Users report increased satisfaction during critical times (Target: reduction in crisis escalations by **30%**).

**User Story 4: Integrated Therapeutic Support**

- *As a therapist, I want a dashboard that provides holistic oversight of client interactions to intervene when necessary.* - **Acceptance Criteria**: - The dashboard displays real-time interaction metrics and flags cases for review. - Therapists report a **15% increase** in satisfaction with support tools.

---

## 4. Technical Considerations and Constraints

- **Cloud Infrastructure**: Utilize a cloud-based, microservices architecture (AWS/Azure) to enable horizontal scaling and high availability.- **Real-Time Data Processing**: Implement RESTful APIs with a machine learning pipeline for real-time language understanding and personalization.- **Security & HIPAA Compliance**: Ensure end-to-end encryption, multi-factor authentication, and policies that meet HIPAA regulations.- **Performance Targets**: Achieve a response time of under **2 seconds**, support **10,000+ concurrent users**, and maintain **99.9% uptime**.- **Integration**: Seamlessly integrate with existing therapy session tools and electronic health record systems.

(Source: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach â Between-Session Support, Technical Architecture Section)

---

## 5. Success Metrics and KPIs for Measuring Impact

| **Metric** | **Current Value** | **Target Value** | **Trend/Gap** ||------------|-------------------|------------------|---------------|| Weekly Active Users | 45% baseline | **80%** | +35% increase needed || Conversation Completion Rate | 72% baseline | **90%** | +18% uplift || Average Session Duration | (Baseline not specified) | **15 minutes** | Target set || Return Rate within 48 Hours | (Baseline not specified) | **85%** | Target set || Therapy Dropout Rate | **32%** | Reduced by 50% (target 16%) | Significant reduction required || Crisis Escalations | (Not quantified) | **30% reduction** | Critical reduction |

*Note: These metrics are supported by the longitudinal analysis indicating a 47% decline in static content usage and a 312% increase in support-seeking behaviors, underlining the urgency of bridging the support gap.* (Source: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach â Between-Session Support)

---

## 6. Pros and Cons Analysis of the AI Coach Therapy Approach

### Pros <SEP> https://source-aggregator-result-s3-bucket.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/f32654c4-48ab-429b-8eae-65b746fe432f/56246c87-c702-4bf4-9912-d7e49292ec0d/d8559b3b-fdb1-4faf-8f68-927d42073be6/72c235b6-b723-4802-99b4-bc00b6dbb7c2j/407ac7b5-a196-4ed0-a32f-277618470493.html <SEP> Title: AI-Powered Therapy Support System PRD - Between-Session Client Engagement

## 4. Technical Considerations and Constraints

- **Cloud Infrastructure**: Utilize a cloud-based, microservices architecture (AWS/Azure) to enable horizontal scaling and high availability.- **Real-Time Data Processing**: Implement RESTful APIs with a machine learning pipeline for real-time language understanding and personalization.- **Security & HIPAA Compliance**: Ensure end-to-end encryption, multi-factor authentication, and policies that meet HIPAA regulations.- **Performance Targets**: Achieve a response time of under **2 seconds**, support **10,000+ concurrent users**, and maintain **99.9% uptime**.- **Integration**: Seamlessly integrate with existing therapy session tools and electronic health record systems.

(Source: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach â Between-Session Support, Technical Architecture Section)

---

## 5. Success Metrics and KPIs for Measuring Impact

| **Metric** | **Current Value** | **Target Value** | **Trend/Gap** ||------------|-------------------|------------------|---------------|| Weekly Active Users | 45% baseline | **80%** | +35% increase needed || Conversation Completion Rate | 72% baseline | **90%** | +18% uplift || Average Session Duration | (Baseline not specified) | **15 minutes** | Target set || Return Rate within 48 Hours | (Baseline not specified) | **85%** | Target set || Therapy Dropout Rate | **32%** | Reduced by 50% (target 16%) | Significant reduction required || Crisis Escalations | (Not quantified) | **30% reduction** | Critical reduction |

*Note: These metrics are supported by the longitudinal analysis indicating a 47% decline in static content usage and a 312% increase in support-seeking behaviors, underlining the urgency of bridging the support gap.* (Source: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach â Between-Session Support)

---

## 6. Pros and Cons Analysis of the AI Coach Therapy Approach

### Pros

- **24/7 Availability**: Offers constant support, filling the gap when therapists are not available (critical for the 78% of users feeling unsupported during evening hours).- **Personalized Interaction**: Leverages session notes and adaptive NLP to tailor responses, potentially increasing engagement and homework completion rates.- **Data-Driven Insights**: Integrates extensive tracking and progress monitoring, enabling therapists to intervene proactively through an oversight dashboard.

### Cons

- **Perceived Lack of Empathy**: Users might feel that the AI does not genuinely understand their nuanced emotional states, potentially reducing trust and therapeutic alliance (*"I often feel stuck when practicing new coping skills between sessions..."*).- **Algorithmic Limitations**: The effectiveness of personalized responses relies on the AI's training data and algorithm, which may not capture cultural and individual subtleties fully.- **User Adoption Risk**: If the AI fails to resonate on an emotional level, metrics such as conversation completion rates and return rates may lag behind targets, impacting overall treatment outcomes.

(Source: [CLIENT_B] AI Coach â Between-Session Support, Client Testimonials)

---

## Conclusion

The enhanced PRD outlines a comprehensive plan to implement an AI coach designed to provide immediate, personalized, and 24/7 support between therapy sessions. While the approach holds promise in addressing critical engagement gaps, it is essential to mitigate risks related to perceived empathy and user trust. Continuous monitoring against **key success metrics** and leveraging real user feedback will be pivotal in refining the system to meet both clinical and user experience goals.

stories.story_title: Teletherapy Platform Enhancement: Addressing Core User Friction Points
stories.story_full:
# Executive Summary: Customer Pain Points & Solution - Frequency Analysis

## Situation Overview
[CLIENT_B]'s teletherapy platform is experiencing rapid growth with 38% of sessions now conducted virtually, amid increasing demand for mental health services. While the platform shows strong fundamentals with a 78% therapy session completion rate, key challenges persist around user onboarding and technical reliability.

> "The virtual sessions work great for my busy schedule. I appreciate being able to see my therapist from home." - James T., Client

## Key Findings

- **Onboarding Friction Points**
Internal data reveals a 40% drop-off between initial assessment and first appointment booking. This is compounded by provider availability challenges.
> "I wish there were more therapists available in my area. It took a while to find someone who was accepting new patients." - Sarah M., Client

- **Technical Infrastructure Challenges**
Platform stability metrics show strong uptime, but video quality issues are impacting therapist confidence. Documentation indicates video-related support tickets need a 50% reduction target.
> "The billing process was confusing at first..." - Michael R., Client

- **Provider Administrative Burden**
Therapists report significant time spent on post-session documentation, with internal analysis suggesting a potential 30% reduction in administrative time through AI-assisted note-taking implementation.
> "I've had two sessions so far... The therapist matching process was quick and I'm pleased with my provider." - Corrine C., Client

## Strategic Implications
The platform's current 4.5-month average user retention presents an opportunity for improvement. With telehealth adoption accelerating and increasing demand from millennials and Gen Z, addressing these pain points could drive the targeted 25% increase in therapist adoption and improve the projected 20,000 monthly new user sign-ups goal.

## Recommendations
1. Implement AI-assisted note-taking system to reduce therapist administrative burden
2. Enhance video infrastructure with additional load balancing measures
3. Streamline the therapist availability matching algorithm to reduce initial booking friction

## Risk Considerations
Primary risks include potential resistance to AI-assisted tools from traditional practitioners and temporary service disruptions during technical upgrades. Mitigation requires comprehensive provider training and staged implementation of new features.
